scope_boundary.authorized_runtime_scope: local lab / intentionally vulnerable app only

owasp_reference.project: OWASP Top 10 Web Application Security Risks
owasp_reference.release_cadence_note: OWASP Top 10 is not published every calendar year; testing-plan coverage tracks every official release edition instead of nonexistent annual lists.

oss_recon_references.name: OWASP Top 10 / WSTG
oss_recon_references.useful_pattern: Taxonomy, testing objectives, and verification checklists
oss_recon_references.safety_note: Checklist items are not confirmed vulnerabilities without manual verification

oss_recon_references.name: OWASP ZAP baseline/passive alert model
oss_recon_references.safety_note: Do not copy active scan defaults or scanner-confirmed semantics

oss_recon_references.name: Nuclei templates
oss_recon_references.useful_pattern: Template ID, severity, tags, references, matcher/extractor concept
oss_recon_references.safety_note: Do not execute templates directly without scope, tag allowlist, and T4 review

oss_recon_references.name: ProjectDiscovery httpx/katana
oss_recon_references.useful_pattern: Structured JSONL observations and composable tool boundaries

oss_recon_references.name: SARIF / DefectDojo concepts
oss_recon_references.safety_note: Do not overfit or auto-promote triage observations

categories.name: Broken Access Control
categories.safe_start: offline/planned auth and route matrix from fixtures
categories.runtime_posture: T4 only for narrow approved local-lab workflows; no auth bypass automation by default

categories.name: Cryptographic Failures
categories.safe_start: passive metadata and header/cookie flag observation

categories.name: Injection
categories.safe_start: planned workflow and fixture-only parser tests
categories.runtime_posture: T4 for benign-marker local-lab checks; no exploit payload chains

categories.name: Security Misconfiguration
categories.safe_start: headers, directory listing metadata, robots/security.txt, API docs metadata

categories.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
categories.safe_start: package/dependency manifest presence and version metadata only
categories.runtime_posture: T1/T2; no recursive downloads or private artifact collection

categories.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
categories.runtime_posture: T0/T3 planned first; no brute force or credential stuffing

categories.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
categories.safe_start: lab report/evidence checklist and defensive observation notes

categories.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
categories.runtime_posture: T3 planned only; T4/T5 if callbacks or network pivots are ever proposed

candidate_output_policy.evidence_policy: metadata, hashes, headers, status, short redacted snippets only; no raw secrets or loot

official_release_coverage.coverage_policy: Include every official OWASP Top 10 web-application release edition in the test-plan traceability matrix; do not invent per-calendar-year Top 10 lists.
official_release_coverage.primary_runtime_taxonomy: 2025 latest release once local category mapping is added; keep 2021 mapping until migration is reviewed.
official_release_coverage.historical_release_usage: Use historical editions for regression/traceability, renamed/merged-risk mapping, and missed-class gap analysis; runtime modules still require their own safety tier and run card.
not_in_scope: creating fake yearly OWASP lists for years without an official release
auto-running tests solely because a historical category exists
public or real bug-bounty target execution without separate scope/rules approval
